Monday: Margaret opened with "Jalapeño corn dog bites. We're doing it this week and I need to know right..." - questioning whether the dish had real technique behind it. Tuesday: Margaret confirmed a 60-40 cornmeal-to-flour minimum after testing - "Marcus gets it. Fine. Cornmeal-to-flour ratio is 60-40 minimum or the seal breaks down in the oil." Marcus framed this as a performance threshold, not a limitation. Stephanie agreed the copy should anchor on crust performance rather than ingredients. All three aligned on shipping.

Wednesday: Julian presented three options for the bite-through shot. Margaret agreed, noting the crisp exterior matters more than the jalapeno cross-section. Shot two locked in unanimously.

Thursday: Marcus brought finalized copy focusing on cornmeal's moisture-seal function. Margaret clarified the mechanic - cornmeal absorbs steam before it hits the batter - and pushed to lead with that over taste. Stephanie said "Love that you went all in on the moisture-seal thing - let's see what you've got?" then pushed for tighter structure. Margaret approved Marcus's streamlined version: lead with moisture arrest, show texture results, skip over-explaining.

Friday: Margaret opened with "Final review. Shot two locked, copy locked. Someone tell me what isn't right, because something..." - expecting problems. Marcus pushed back, saying trusting the work improved it. The group agreed restraint drove success: Marcus credited a science-first approach for stronger copy; Julian said minimal angles made shot two land. No disagreements surfaced.

Saturday: This conversation contains no discussion of "Jalapeno Corn Dog Bites" - no ingredients, techniques, or food ideas were mentioned beyond a passing metaphor.
